subject: [PATCH v3 7/7] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru - [s390] panic in __memcg_list_lru_alloc

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the initialization of list_lru structures in THP shrinker, acknowledged that the existing callsites had their own memcg_kmem_online() guards, and proposed a fix by adding a guard to __list_lru_init().
reviews.raw_body:
Can you verify whether the kdump kernel boots with
cgroup_disable=memory?

I think there is an issue with how we call __list_lru_init(). The
existing callsites had their own memcg_kmem_online() guards. But the
THP one does not, so we're creating a memcg-aware list_lru, but the
do-while hierarchy walk in __memcg_list_lru_alloc() runs into a NULL
memcg.

Can you try the below on top of that -next checkout?

diff --git a/mm/list_lru.c b/mm/list_lru.c
index 1ccdd45b1d14..7c7024e33653 100644
--- a/mm/list_lru.c
+++ b/mm/list_lru.c
@@ -637,7 +637,7 @@ int __list_lru_init(struct list_lru *lru, bool memcg_aware, struct shrinker *shr
 	else
 		lru->shrinker_id = -1;
 
-	if (mem_cgroup_kmem_disabled())
+	if (mem_cgroup_disabled() || mem_cgroup_kmem_disabled())
 		memcg_aware = false;
 #endif

2026-03-30.patch_summary: This patch addresses a panic issue on s390 architecture caused by an uninitialized list_lru structure in the deferred split shrinker. To debug this, the developer requests additional information from Mikhail Zaslonko, including correlating the function offset to C code and extracting the instruction stream sequence surrounding the crash. The goal is to understand which pointer or data structure is causing the issue.

reviews.author: Vasily Gorbik
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the s390 panic in __memcg_list_lru_alloc is similar to x86 crashes when cgroup_disable=memory is set, and suggested that the root cause may have already been found by Johannes Weiner on x86
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, that is the only special thing about that dump kernel.
Sorry for not including the command line. x86 crashes more or less the
same way with cgroup_disable=memory. But while I was doing the repro on
x86, you already found the root cause.

2026-03-31.patch_summary: This patch addresses a panic issue on s390 architecture caused by an uninitialized list_lru structure in the deferred split shrinker. To debug this, the developer requests additional information from Mikhail Zaslonko, including correlating the function offset to C code and extracting the instruction stream sequence surrounding the crash. The goal is to understand which pointer or data structure is causing the issue.
